title: On The Death Of A Lap-Dog, Named Echo.
content:
In wood and wild, ye warbling throng,
            Your heavy loss deplore;
        Now half extinct your powers of song,
            Sweet Echo is no more.

        Ye jarring, screeching things around,
            Scream your discordant joys;
        Now half your din of tuneless sound
            With Echo silent lies.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Burns, titled "On The Death Of A Lap-Dog, Named Echo.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
